channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Rubio says U.S. combat operations in Iran are over as focus shifts to negotiations
item.description: The Trump administration is in a new full-court press to message optimism on the unpopular war in Iran.
item.content:encoded: The Trump administration is in a new full-court press to message optimism on the unpopular war in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez

item.title: Former U.S. special representative for Iran on Trump pausing 'Project Freedom'
item.description: NPR's A Martínez asks former U.S. special representative for Iran Robert Malley about President Trump's about-face on a brief American effort to guide ships through the Strait of Hormuz.
item.content:encoded: NPR's A Martínez asks former U.S. special representative for Iran Robert Malley about President Trump's about-face on a brief American effort to guide ships through the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Fighting in southern Lebanon continues despite Israeli-Lebanese ceasefire
item.description: As the ceasefire with Iran comes under strain in the Gulf, the ceasefire in southern Lebanon is also fraying.
item.content:encoded: As the ceasefire with Iran comes under strain in the Gulf, the ceasefire in southern Lebanon is also fraying.
item.dc:creator: Jane Arraf

item.title: Poll: Trump blamed for gas prices as Democrats gain midterm edge
item.description: A new NPR/PBS News/Marist poll finds strong support for Democrats this midterm season. It also shows increasing concern about the war in Iran and the economy, particularly the price of gas.
item.content:encoded:
A new NPR/PBS News/Marist poll finds strong support for Democrats this midterm season. It also shows increasing concern about the war in Iran and the economy, particularly the price of gas.

(Image credit: Spencer Platt)
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro

item.title: A trip to Europe? In this economy? Expensive flights keep vacations closer to home
item.description: Travel advisers are seeing Americans picking cheaper domestic travel destinations over Europe.
item.content:encoded:
Travel advisers are seeing Americans picking cheaper domestic travel destinations over Europe.

(Image credit: Stephan Bisaha)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: Legal scholar Kimberlé Crenshaw talks about her new book, 'Backtalker'
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with legal theorist Kimberlé Crenshaw about her new memoir "Backtalker."
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with legal theorist Kimberlé Crenshaw about her new memoir "Backtalker."
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Napheesa Collier says her critique of the WNBA was 'a little dramatic,' but it worked
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks WNBA star Napheesa Collier about the league's new contract with the players and the state of women's sports.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks WNBA star Napheesa Collier about the league's new contract with the players and the state of women's sports.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Rubio says U.S. combat operations in Iran are over as focus shifts to negotiations, takeaways from Tuesday's primaries in Ohio and Indiana, poll finds Democrats are in a strong position for midterms.
item.content:encoded: Rubio says U.S. combat operations in Iran are over as focus shifts to negotiations, takeaways from Tuesday's primaries in Ohio and Indiana, poll finds Democrats are in a strong position for midterms.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Tornado outbreaks catch forecasters by surprise after National Weather Service cuts
item.description: Staffing cuts forced the National Weather Service to cut early morning weather balloon launches. Then two tornado outbreaks this spring caught forecasters by surprise.
item.content:encoded: Staffing cuts forced the National Weather Service to cut early morning weather balloon launches. Then two tornado outbreaks this spring caught forecasters by surprise.
item.dc:creator: Frank Morris

item.title: U.S. stages largest-ever annual military drills in the Philippines
item.description: U.S. and allies hold their largest drills yet in the Philippines near Taiwan, showcasing new tactics and alliances to deter China—while Beijing stages its own exercises nearby.
item.content:encoded: U.S. and allies hold their largest drills yet in the Philippines near Taiwan, showcasing new tactics and alliances to deter China—while Beijing stages its own exercises nearby.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Kuhn
